options.text: Testing whether human achievement can provide lasting meaning apart from God

Because they are sinful in themselves
Because only kings may enjoy them

options.text: God has placed eternity in the human heart while limiting human understanding of His complete purposes

options.text: Because temporary gifts are gracious provisions from God amid life's uncertainties

options.text: Wealth can never satisfy the deepest longings of the human heart

options.text: Perfect justice always exists in this life

options.text: Wisdom is both profoundly valuable and yet unable to solve every mystery of life

options.text: To encourage generous, faithful action despite uncertain outcomes

options.text: Humans can predict the future accurately

Because old people cannot serve God
Because wisdom belongs only to the young

options.text: The gradual decline of the human body with aging and death

options.text: Because reverence for God gives meaning to everything else in life
Because only priests should obey God

options.text: It assures that life's apparent injustices will not have the final word

questions.question: What tension dominates the entire book?

options.text: The contrast between life's temporary nature and God's eternal reality

options.text: Nothing in creation has any value whatsoever

options.text: Lasting meaning is found only by living in reverence and obedience before God

questions.question: What is the central message of Ecclesiastes?

options.text: Earthly pursuits alone cannot satisfy; true meaning is found in fearing God and receiving His gifts with gratitude

questions.question: What question does Ecclesiastes ultimately answer?

options.text: Where can lasting meaning be found in a temporary world?

It is merely a repeated formula

questions.question: What does the healing at the Beautiful Gate reveal?

options.text: Jesus continues His ministry through His empowered witnesses

questions.question: Why did the religious leaders oppose resurrection preaching?

options.text: It challenged their authority and proclaimed Jesus as the final authority

questions.question: What does “there is none other name under heaven” emphasize?

options.text: Salvation is uniquely found through Jesus

questions.question: Why is Barnabas introduced in Acts?

options.text: He represents generosity, encouragement, and partnership in gospel mission

questions.question: What does Ananias and Sapphira’s story reveal about the early church?

options.text: God takes holiness and truthfulness seriously even in a grace-filled community

questions.question: Why did Gamaliel advise caution toward the apostles?

options.text: He recognized that opposition to a work of God could ultimately oppose God Himself
He believed Jesus was definitely false

questions.question: Why is Stephen’s death important in Acts?

options.text: It becomes a catalyst for persecution and the spread of the gospel beyond Jerusalem

questions.question: What does Stephen seeing Jesus standing at God’s right hand reveal?

options.text: Jesus shares divine authority and receives His faithful witnesses

questions.question: Why was Philip’s ministry in Samaria significant?

options.text: It demonstrated the gospel crossing historic Jewish-Samaritan boundaries

questions.question: What was wrong with Simon the sorcerer’s request?

options.text: He viewed God’s gift as something that could be purchased and controlled

questions.question: Why was Saul’s conversion so significant?

options.text: God transformed a persecutor into a chosen messenger for the nations

questions.question: What does Peter’s vision of clean and unclean animals teach?

options.text: God was revealing that Gentiles could be accepted into His people

questions.question: Why is Cornelius important?

options.text: He represents the inclusion of Gentiles into God’s salvation through faith

questions.question: What was the major turning point in Acts?

options.text: The gospel officially expands from a Jewish-centered movement to a worldwide mission

questions.question: What is the main role of the Holy Spirit in Acts?

options.text: Empowering believers to witness Christ and advance God’s kingdom

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes Acts?

options.text: The risen Christ continues His mission through the Holy Spirit, empowering His church to take the gospel to all nations.

Acts describes the creation of a political kingdom.
Acts shows the church separating from God’s promises.

questions.question: Why should young women love their husbands and be keepers at home?

options.text: That the word of God be not blasphemed

questions.question: In doctrine, Titus is to show uncorruptness, gravity, sincerity, and sound speech that cannot be what?
Why should servants show good fidelity, that they may do what to the doctrine of God?

options.text: Adorn the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things

questions.question: The grace of God that brings salvation has appeared to how many?
With what authority is Titus to speak, exhort, and rebuke?

options.text: With all authority; let no man despise him

questions.question: How does Paul describe our former state before God's kindness appeared?

options.text: Foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving divers lusts, hateful

questions.question: What appeared that moved God to save us, according to Titus 3:4?

options.text: The kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man

questions.question: How abundantly was the Holy Ghost shed on us through Jesus Christ our Saviour?
Why does Paul affirm these things constantly, that believers maintain good works?

options.text: These things are good and profitable unto men

questions.question: A heretic rejected after admonitions is described as being what?

options.text: Subverted, and sinneth, being condemned of himself

questions.question: Where did Paul determine to winter, asking Titus to come to him?
Paul calls himself in the salutation a servant of God and an apostle of whom?
Paul's apostleship is according to the faith of God's elect and the acknowledging of the truth which is after what?
God manifested his word through preaching committed to Paul according to whose commandment?
The purpose of ordaining elders in every city was to accomplish what Paul had appointed?

options.text: Set in order the things that are wanting

questions.question: A bishop must not be given to wine, no striker, and not given to what?
Paul says one of the Cretians, a prophet of their own, said this; how does Paul rate the testimony?
Titus is to warn them not to give heed to Jewish fables and commandments of men that do what?
Aged men are to be sound in faith, in charity, in what?
The aged women likewise are to be in behaviour as becometh holiness, not false accusers, not given to much what?
Titus is to show himself in all things a pattern of what?
The purpose of servants' good conduct is that the adversary may be ashamed, having no what?

options.text: Evil thing to say of them

questions.question: The grace teaches us to live godly in this what world?
Christ purified unto himself a peculiar people that are zealous of what?
Believers are to be ready to every good work, to speak evil of no man, and to be what?

options.text: Gentle, showing all meekness unto all men

questions.question: Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he did what?
The renewing that saves is described as the washing of regeneration and the renewing of whom?
Paul says this is a faithful saying, and these things he wills Titus to affirm how?
The reason to reject a heretic after two admonitions is that such a man is what?
Paul closes urging that ours also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they be not what?
The epistle's closing benediction is which?

options.text: The Lord be with your spirit
Grace be with you all. Amen
